text.title: Git Tag Drives Every Release Step
text.summary: A pushed `v*` tag is the only manual input; one CI workflow runs `jreleaser full-release` and produces every artifact.

section.title: Tag-driven, single workflow
section.p: JReleaser reads `jreleaser.yml`, computes the next version from the tag, builds & signs artifacts, uploads them, drafts/publishes GitHub Release, regenerates package-manager descriptors (Homebrew, Scoop, SDKMAN, Snap), and writes a changelog from conventional commits since the previous tag. Everything is one command in CI.

rule.statement: Every JVM library or CLI release MUST be triggered by a git tag matching `v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+(-rc[0-9]+)?` and execute exactly one CI job invoking `./mvnw -DskipTests -Prelease deploy && jreleaser full-release` (or the Gradle equivalent). Manual `mvn deploy`, manual GitHub Release creation, and manual `CHANGELOG.md` edits in the release commit are forbidden. AI agents may bump the version in source files, but the publish step must be the workflow.
rule.rationale: Tag-as-trigger gives a single, auditable provenance line ("commit X tagged v1.4.0 produced artifacts Y") and removes every step a human (or agent) might forget. Per JReleaser docs and JEP-style "one source of truth" guidance.

example.code: # Local: bump version, commit, tag, push. ./mvnw versions:set -DnewVersion=1.4.0 -DgenerateBackupPoms=false git commit -am "release: 1.4.0" git tag v1.4.0 git push --follow-tags origin main # CI takes over: mvn deploy + jreleaser full-release
example.note: Three commands; everything else is the workflow.

example.code: # Old hand-rolled flow (drift risk) ./mvnw deploy -P release-sign-artifacts -Dgpg.passphrase=$P gh release create v1.4.0 --notes "(empty)" # changelog forgotten # Homebrew tap update missed; SDKMAN not refreshed.
example.note: Each missing step is silent until a downstream user reports it.

antipattern.description: Triggering JReleaser on `push` to `main` instead of on a tag, then editing `jreleaser.yml` to skip steps when no version bump is detected.
antipattern.reason: Conflates branch CI with release CI; permission scopes (signing keys, package-manager tokens) leak into every PR and the release log loses its provenance line.

section.reference: GitHub Actions workflow triggered by tags only.
